mnemonics: My OSPF Supports Packets For Everyone (MOSPF)
keyPoints: MOSPF is an extension to OSPF (Open Shortest Path First) that adds multicast routing capabilities.
It uses the existing OSPF link-state database to build source-based multicast distribution trees for each (source, group) pair.
It is most efficient within a single OSPF area, as inter-area multicast routing requires special design considerations.
commonMistakes: Confusing MOSPF with PIM (Protocol Independent Multicast), which is a separate and more widely used multicast routing protocol.
Assuming MOSPF is efficient for wide-area or inter-domain multicast; it was designed for and is best suited to a single, well-managed OSPF domain.
